title: betula alba subsp. papyrifera
intro: Betula alba subsp. papyrifera, also known as paper birch, is a subspecies of the white birch tree that is native to North America. This deciduous tree is valued for its bark, which has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of paper birch have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the bark is often made into a tea or tincture, or used in topical applications such as salves and creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
